item.title: Iran, U.S. ramp up tit-for-tat strikes ahead of slain leader's burial
item.description: Iran's former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei is being buried amid an escalating exchange of fire on the 132nd day of the war.

item.title: Jet fuel prices have dropped. Why are air fares still so high?
item.description: Lower jet fuel costs spell relief for airlines. But that doesn't mean they'll pass savings onto customers by lowering airfares, experts said.

item.title: Questions arise over new Air Force One after Trump uses old plane
item.description: The Secret Service advised that President Trump take the old plane to depart Turkey, according to people briefed on the situation.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $12,000 high-yield savings account can earn now
item.description: Have $12,000 in a traditional savings account? Here's how much interest you'd earn with a high-yield account instead.

item.title: Prosecutors to play interview with Tyler Robinson's roommate in Charlie Kirk trial
item.description: Video of an interview with the roommate of Tyler Robinson, the suspect in the killing of Charlie Kirk, is set to be played in court Thursday. Robinson allegedly confessed to killing Kirk in a conversation with the roommate. CBS News correspondent Carter Evans reports.

item.title: U.S. star Christian Pulisic fractured leg in World Cup loss to Belgium
item.description: Christian Pulisic was diagnosed with a fractured right leg, the U.S. Soccer Federation announced Thursday.

item.title: Inside Trump's recent dealings with Iran, NATO, Ukraine
item.description: President Trump wrapped the NATO summit in Turkey on a positive note Wednesday, appearing more favorable towards Ukraine than usual. CBS News contributor Niall Ferguson joins to discuss the summit, Mr. Trump's latest actions in Iran and the Ukraine-Russia war.

item.title: North Texas aviation pioneer Wally Funk dies, aged 87
item.description: Wally Funk was selected in 1961 as one of the elite women in the Mercury 13 program, finishing the same rigorous training program given to the Mercury 7 astronauts.

item.title: Trump says Iran wants to make a deal "so badly" as strikes continue
item.description: The U.S. launched a new wave of strikes against Iran Wednesday as President Trump declared the ceasefire to be over. About 90 military targets were hit, U.S. CENTCOM says. Mr. Trump claims Iran wants to make a deal, but timing is unclear. Olivia Rinaldi reports for CBS.

item.title: Irish citizen found guilty of murdering American woman in Hungary
item.description: Investigators said Mackenzie Michalski and the suspect met at a nightclub and danced before leaving for the man's rented apartment.

item.title: Here's what happens after Graham Platner ended his Senate campaign
item.description: Graham Platner dropped out of the Maine Senate race Wednesday amid multiple sexual assault allegations, which he denies. Zak Hudak breaks down what happens next for CBS News.

item.title: American-made technology guiding Ukraine's strikes into Russia
item.description: The American defense firm Shield AI has played an increasingly important role in Ukraine's campaign of strikes in Russia.

item.title: Can creditors put a lien on inherited property if you have unpaid debt?
item.description: Have you inherited real estate while carrying debt? Here's what your creditors can and can't do to collect on it.

item.title: Olympian David Hearn pleads not guilty to charges of damaging Reflecting Pool
item.description: Three-time Olympian David Hearn was indicted July 2, after he was accused of ripping out a portion of the sealant from the bottom of the Reflecting Pool on June 19.

item.title: Israeli strike kills aid worker who brought World Cup screenings to Gaza
item.description: An Israeli strike has killed an aid worker who organized World Cup game screenings for Palestinians in the war-torn Gaza Strip, a hospital director says.

item.title: Police investigating after missing teen Nolan Wells found dead on Mississippi island
item.description: Nolan Wells was found dead on Horn Island in Mississippi after the teen vanished during a Fourth of July trip with friends. CBS News' Skyler Henry has the latest details.

item.title: Iran ramps up Strait of Hormuz threats amid renewed U.S. tensions
item.description: Iran renewed its threats to close the Strait of Hormuz as tensions re-escalated following President Trump's announcement that the U.S.-Iran ceasefire ended. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more.

item.title: Venezuelans reunite with pets rescued by volunteers after deadly earthquakes
item.description: Cleanup and recovery efforts are underway after two deadly earthquakes devastated Venezuela, destroying homes and killing thousands. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez spoke with animal rescue volunteers working to save pets and ignite hope.

item.title: Iran and U.S. have entered "new phase" of war, H.R. McMaster says
item.description: The U.S. and Iran renewed strikes despite President Trump's claim that Tehran wants to make a deal. Retired Lt. Gen. H.R. McMaster joins to discuss the current state of the Iran war.

item.title: Oil prices seesaw after renewed tensions between U.S. and Iran
item.description: Oil prices are reacting to the re-escalation of the conflict between the U.S. and Iran. CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger has more.

item.title: Trump claims Iran "wants to make a deal so badly" after more strikes
item.description: President Trump told reporters early Thursday that Tehran "wants to make a deal so badly" after the U.S. and Iran traded more strikes. CBS News Olivia Rinaldi and Courtney Kealy have more.

item.title: Democrats scramble to find replacement after Graham Platner drops out of Maine Senate race
item.description: Democrat Graham Platner suspended his Senate campaign in Maine following a sexual assault allegation, which he vehemently denied. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns has more on the news, and political consultant Kristian Ramos also joins to discuss.

item.title: Army to share findings of Kuwait attack probe with Gold Star families
item.description: Six Americans were killed in the March 1 Iranian strike on a tactical operations center in Kuwait.

item.title: 2026 Emmy nominations are announced: See the full list
item.description: The nominations for the 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards were announced Wednesday morning in Los Angeles, with the final season of HBO Max's "Hacks" setting a new record for the most nominations in a single year for a comedy series.

item.title: Can You Hack Your Brain to Stop Impulse Buying? | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
item.description:
"Dopamine sites" are on the rise in South Korea, allowing people to simulate the thrill of online shopping or food delivery without spending any actual money. Could this be the fix for compulsive spenders? Plus, Jill helps caller Danielle navigate a financial crossroads: after taking a pay cut to make work and life more manageable, she's now facing higher-than-expected college costs for her child while trying to stay on track for retirement.
Have a money question? Email askjill@jillonmoney.com.

item.title: Bonnie Tyler, who sang "Total Eclipse of the Heart," dies at 75
item.description: Bonnie Tyler, the Welsh pop star best known for singing the chart-topping power ballad "Total Eclipse of the Heart" in 1983, has died. She was 75.

item.title: Some large ICE facilities haven't been inspected in over a year
item.description: Detention facilities are now inspected every year or every other year rather than twice a year, a CBS News analysis of inspection reports found.

item.title: Read full episode transcripts of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" from 2026
item.description: Browse all of our full episode transcripts from 2026 of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan."

item.title: Disturbing details emerge after U.S. woman killed in Budapest
item.description: Investigators said Mackenzie Michalski and the suspect met at a nightclub and danced before leaving for the man's rented apartment.

item.title: American nurse killed in Budapest while on vacation, police arrest suspect
item.description: The victim, Mackenzie Michalski was reported missing on Nov. 5 after she was last seen at a nightclub in central Budapest.
